objective: Build a local-first incident workflow capsule for incident-hub
current_understanding: Project already has incident lifecycle, dedupe, escalation, timeline, and tests
Need a believable AI-developer handoff for later agents
constraints: Keep history append-only and small but realistic

decision: Model the incident lifecycle explicitly as NEW -> ACKNOWLEDGED -> ESCALATED -> RESOLVED
rationale: The lifecycle is the core source of truth for acknowledgements and state transitions; keeping it explicit makes the workflow understandable for the next agent

q: Should acknowledged incidents ever auto-escalate on idle time, or only unacknowledged incidents?

note: Confirmed the implementation keeps escalation policy severity-based and idle-time driven, while excluding acknowledged and resolved incidents from automatic escalation. This matches the tests around AUTO_ESCALATABLE_STATES and manual escalation paths.

answer: Only NEW and ESCALATED incidents auto-escalate. ACKNOWLEDGED incidents stay manual because ownership has been assigned and the operator should control when escalation happens.

decision: Treat the timeline as the audit log and derive postmortem metrics from timeline entries at render time
rationale: This keeps time-to-acknowledge, time-to-resolve, and alert counts consistent with the actual event history instead of maintaining parallel counters

note: Verified the package shape: dedupe is service+fingerprint, acknowledgements set owner, escalation reuses a shared helper for manual and automatic bumps, and report rendering walks the incident list for a postmortem view.

objective: Hand off incident-hub with a clear continuity note and one follow-up item
current_understanding: Timeline output is already the postmortem surface
Current tests cover lifecycle, dedupe, escalation, hub orchestration, and timeline formatting
continuity: This capsule picks up after the lifecycle and escalation rules were stabilized; the next agent should focus on finish-quality issues rather than reworking the domain model.

note: Prepared capsule artifacts only. No code changes were required in incident_hub/, so the service implementation remains untouched.

q: Should the next pass add a note event type or leave the timeline kinds fixed to the current postmortem set?

note: Next-agent follow-up: decide whether `TimelineKind.NOTE` should be surfaced in report rendering and tests, or remain reserved for future incident annotations.
